slices.focus: Housing project delays and trust crisis management
slices.summary: Contractor qualification and property deed mismatches stalled multiple household files, triggering a community trust crisis. After two difficult public meetings absorbing resident frustration, developed a structured communication approach: brief timeline apology without full admission, prioritized phone calls to affected households, and a shift from defensive retrospective to clear next-step framing for each stalled case.
open_loops: Whether overall community trust can recover after the cumulative timeline delays
How to balance individual household calls and collective meetings under tight resource constraints
Whether the defensive emotional tendency triggered by public criticism will affect future work judgment
decisions: Give a brief timeline apology without making a full admission of fault that could escalate liability
Prioritize direct phone calls to scope-recheck households before holding public meetings
Use 'real blocker plus specific next step' frame instead of retrospective defense in public communication
